query: IMD monsoon onset Kerala May 2026 press release declaration status
answer:
Excellent! I now have a comprehensive picture. Let me compile my findings.

---

## IMD Monsoon Onset Over Kerala — May 2026: Research Report

### 1. Current Declaration Status (as of May 20, 2026)

**The formal monsoon onset over Kerala has NOT yet been declared by IMD.** As of May 20, 2026, the monsoon has not yet arrived over the Kerala coast. IMD has issued a **forecast** for the expected onset date, but no formal declaration of arrival has been made.

### 2. IMD's Onset Forecast (Issued May 15–16, 2026)

On **May 15–16, 2026**, IMD issued its operational forecast for monsoon onset over Kerala:

> *"This year, the southwest (summer) monsoon is likely to set in over Kerala on **May 26** with a model error of **± 4 days**."*

This means IMD expects onset somewhere in the window of **May 22 to May 30, 2026**, with May 26 as the central estimate. This would represent an **early onset**, approximately 6 days ahead of the climatological normal date of **June 1**.

### 3. Current Monsoon Advance Position (as of May 19, 2026)

Per the IMD All India Weather Summary & Forecast Bulletin reported on **May 19, 2026**:

- The southwest monsoon has **advanced over**:
  - Larger parts of the **southeast Arabian Sea**
  - **Comorin area** (southern tip of India)
  - **Southwest and southeast Bay of Bengal**
  - Most parts of the **Andaman Sea**
  - **All parts of Andaman & Nicobar Islands**
- Conditions are favorable for further advance over the next 3–4 days
- The monsoon is "approaching much earlier than expected"

### 4. Key Contextual Facts

| Metric | Value | Source |
|--------|-------|--------|
| Normal onset date (Kerala) | **June 1** (±7 days std dev) | IMD climatology |
| 2026 forecast onset date | **May 26 ± 4 days** | IMD press release, May 15–16 |
| 2025 actual onset date | **May 24** (8 days early) | IMD / TOI |
| 2026 seasonal rainfall forecast | **~92% of Long Period Average** ("below normal") | IMD |
| ENSO status | **El Niño developing** (WMO: high confidence, May–July 2026) | WMO Global Seasonal Climate Update |
| IMD onset forecast track record | Correct in 20 of 21 years (2005–2025); only miss was 2015 | IMD |

### 5. Broader Seasonal Context

- IMD has forecast **"below normal" monsoon rainfall** for 2026 — the first such outlook in three years — amid growing **El Niño risk**.
- The **World Meteorological Organization (WMO)** flagged high confidence in El Niño onset by mid-2026, with models indicating it could be a "strong event."
- Rainfall is forecast at approximately **92% of the Long Period Average (LPA)**.
- Heavy pre-monsoon rainfall was already impacting **Northeast India** by May 16–17, with over 6,000 people affected by flooding in Assam.
- Kerala districts (Ernakulam, Idukki, Malappuram, Kozhikode, Kannur, Kasaragod) were on **yellow alert** for heavy rain and thunderstorms as of mid-May.
- As of May 19, the **Ernakulam district administration** was stepping up "pre-monsoon" preparedness, indicating the monsoon had not yet formally arrived.

### 6. Timeline of Key Events

| Date | Event |
|------|-------|
| April 28, 2026 | WMO flags El Niño onset by May–July 2026; IMD warns of below-normal monsoon (~92% LPA) |
| May 14, 2026 | IMD: Conditions becoming favorable for monsoon advance over south Bay of Bengal, Andaman Sea, and Andaman & Nicobar Islands by May 16 |
| May 15–16, 2026 | **IMD issues onset forecast: Monsoon likely to set in over Kerala on May 26 (±4 days)** |
| May 16, 2026 | Monsoon advancing; pre-monsoon flooding in NE India (Assam) |
| May 19, 2026 | IMD bulletin confirms monsoon has advanced over SE Arabian Sea, Comorin area, Bay of Bengal, Andaman Sea, and all of A&N Islands. Further advance expected in 3–4 days. Kerala on heavy rain/thunderstorm alert. |
| May 19, 2026 | Ernakulam district steps up **pre-monsoon** preparedness (indicating onset not yet declared) |
| **May 20, 2026 (today)** | **No formal onset declaration yet.** Forecast window: May 22–30. |

### 7. Sources

1. **Times of India** — "Monsoon likely to hit Kerala on May 26: IMD" (May 16, 2026) — https://timesofindia.indiatimes.com/india/monsoon-likely-to-hit-kerala-on-may-26-imd/articleshow/131129889.cms
2. **Times of India** — "IMD predicts onset of monsoon over Kerala on May 26" (May 15, 2026) — https://timesofindia.indiatimes.com/india/imd-predicts-onset-of-monsoon-over-kerala-on-may-26/articleshow/131114593.cms
3. **Times of India (Kochi)** — "IMD: Conditions favourable for monsoon onset" (May 14, 2026) — https://timesofindia.indiatimes.com/city/kochi/imd-conditions-favourable-for-monsoon-onset/articleshow/131075485.cms
4. **Times of India** — "Travelling in India this week? IMD issues heavy rain and heatwave warnings" (May 19, 2026) — https://timesofindia.indiatimes.com/life-style/travel/news/travelling-in-india-this-week-imd-issues-heavy-rain-and-heatwave-warnings-across-major-tourist-regions/articleshow/131189711.cms
5. **Times of India (Guwahati)** — "Early monsoon signs bring flood frights to NE; over 6,000 affected" (May 16, 2026) — https://timesofindia.indiatimes.com/city/guwahati/early-monsoon-signs-bring-flood-frights-to-ne-over-6000-affected/articleshow/131144080.cms
6. **Times of India** — "Heatwave grips India: WMO flags El Niño by mid-2026; IMD warns of below-normal monsoon" (April 28, 2026) — https://timesofindia.indiatimes.com/india/heatwave-grips-india-wmo-flags-el-nio-by-mid-2026-imd-warns-of-below-normal-monsoon/articleshow/130570440.cms
7. **The Hindu (Kerala)** — "Ernakulam district administration steps up pre-monsoon preparedness" (May 19, 2026) — https://www.thehindu.com/news/national/kerala/ernakulam-district-administration-steps-up-pre-monsoon-prepardness/article70999140.ece

sources.title: Monsoon likely to hit Kerala on May 26: IMD
sources.snippet: Monsoon is likely to arrive in Kerala on May 26, IMD said Friday, forecasting an early start to the rainy season in India. This year, the southwest monsoon is likely to set in over Kerala on May 26 with a model error of ± 4 days.

sources.title: IMD predicts onset of monsoon over Kerala on May 26
sources.snippet: The monsoon is likely to set in over Kerala on May 26, IMD said on Friday while making a forecast of an early start of the rainy season. Last year, monsoon arrived over Kerala eight days in advance on May 24.

sources.title: IMD: Conditions favourable for monsoon onset
sources.snippet: Conditions are becoming favourable for the arrival of southwest monsoon over parts of south Bay of Bengal, Andaman Sea and Andaman and Nicobar Islands by May 16, IMD said.

sources.title: Travelling in India this week? IMD issues heavy rain and heatwave warnings across major tourist regions
sources.snippet: IMD reported that southwest monsoon has now entered into larger parts of the southeast Arabian sea, Comorin area, southwest and southeast Bay of Bengal, most parts of Andaman sea, and all parts of Andaman and Nicobar islands.

sources.title: Early monsoon signs bring flood frights to NE; over 6,000 affected
sources.snippet: The Southwest Monsoon is likely to set in over Kerala on May 26 with a model error of ± four days. Once the monsoon hits Kerala ahead of schedule, the northeast too can expect an early onset.

sources.title: Heatwave grips India: WMO flags El Niño by mid-2026; IMD warns of below-normal monsoon
sources.snippet: IMD has also forecasted a below-normal southwest monsoon for 2026, the first such outlook in three years, with rainfall likely at about 92% of the long period average.

sources.title: Ernakulam district administration steps up pre-monsoon preparedness
sources.snippet: In the wake of the intensifying monsoon, District Collector has ordered the immediate felling of dangerous trees and pruning of tree branches. A review meeting will be held on May 20.

key_facts: IMD has NOT yet formally declared monsoon onset over Kerala as of May 20, 2026. Only a forecast has been issued.
IMD issued its operational onset forecast on May 15-16, 2026: monsoon is likely to set in over Kerala on May 26 with a model error of ±4 days (window: May 22-30).
The climatological normal date for monsoon onset over Kerala is June 1, with a standard deviation of ~7 days. The 2026 forecast thus projects an early onset (~6 days ahead of normal).
In 2025, monsoon arrived over Kerala on May 24, which was 8 days early.
As of May 19, 2026, the southwest monsoon has advanced over larger parts of the SE Arabian Sea, Comorin area, SW and SE Bay of Bengal, most of the Andaman Sea, and all of Andaman & Nicobar Islands.
IMD's onset forecasts have been correct in 20 of 21 years (2005-2025), with 2015 being the only miss.
IMD has forecast 'below normal' monsoon rainfall for 2026 at ~92% of the Long Period Average, the first below-normal outlook in three years.
WMO has flagged high confidence in El Niño onset by May-July 2026, which is associated with weaker monsoon in South Asia.
Pre-monsoon flooding was already affecting NE India by May 16, with over 6,000 people affected in Assam.
Kerala districts were on yellow alert for heavy rainfall and thunderstorms as of mid-May 2026, and Ernakulam district was stepping up pre-monsoon preparedness as of May 19.
